bundle_metadata.selection_note: A0 core models come from the formal progressive summary baseline and point back to the exact matrix artifact used for that cell.

tool_calls.purpose: repo_export_inventory: Identify code directories, hidden files, data folders, notebooks, outputs, and existing export artifacts
secrets_inventory: Detect secret-bearing filenames without exposing contents
ignore_rule_mismatch: Inspect existing ignore rules to understand current exclusion policy
export_conflict_policy: Check if export.zip already exists
demo_vs_private_asset_inventory and notebook_output_policy: Identify notebooks, checkpoints, caches, and generated outputs

runner_response_json.user_facing_response: I will now inspect your repository to understand its structure, identify any sensitive files, and check what ignore rules are already in place. This will help me ask you only the policy questions that cannot be determined from the workspace itself.
runner_response_json.brief_rationale: Per condition A0_interactive, I must inspect recoverable slots before asking. The repo structure, secrets patterns, existing ignore rules, conflicts, and artifact types are all grounded in the workspace and can be discovered without user input. Only after understanding what exists can I ask targeted policy questions about demo assets, runtime artifacts, and notebook handling that require your judgment.
